path: Structure 1. Models of the particulate nature of matter > Structure 1.1ГўВЂВ”Introduction to the particulate nature of matter > Structure 1.1.1- Elements, compounds and mixtures. Elements are the primary constituents of matter, which cannot be chemically broken down into simpler substances. Compounds consist of atoms of different elements chemically bonded together in a fixed ratio. Mixtures contain more than one element or compound in no fixed ratio, which are not chemically bonded and so can be separated by physical methods. Distinguish between the properties of elements, compounds and mixtures.

text_body:
Which is correct?


A. Mixtures are either homogeneous or heterogeneous and their chemical properties are an average of the individual component properties.

B. Mixtures are never heterogeneous and their chemical properties are an average of the individual component properties.

C. Mixtures are either homogeneous or heterogeneous and the components retain their individual chemical properties.

D. Mixtures are never homogeneous and the components retain their individual chemical properties.

path: Structure 1. Models of the particulate nature of matter > Structure 1.3ГўВЂВ”Electron configurations > Structure 1.3.2ГўВЂВ”The line emission spectrum of the hydrogen atom. text_body:
Which of the following transitions in the hydrogen atom emits the least energy?

A. n = 2 to n = 1

B. n = 3 to n = 1

C. n = 4 to n = 2

D. n = 4 to n = 3

Models of the particulate nature of matter > Structure 1.3ГўВЂВ”Electron configurations > Structure 1.3.5ГўВЂВ”Each orbital has a defined energy state for a given electron configuration and chemical environment, and can hold two electrons of opposite spin. Sublevels contain a fixed number of orbitals, regions of space where there is a high probability of finding an electron. Apply the Aufbau principle, HundГўВЂВ™s rule and the Pauli exclusion principle to deduce electron configurations for atoms and ions up to Z = 36.
text_body:
What is the ground state electron configuration of an atom of chromium, Cr (Z = 24)?

A. [Ar]3d6

B. [Ar]4s23d4

C. [Ar]4s13d5

D. [Ar]4s24p4

due to phenol/hydroxyl/OH group not present in aspirin ГўВњВ”

text_body: Show that, for combustion of equal masses of fuel, ethanol (Mr = 46 g molГўВ€В’1) has a lower carbon footprint than octane (Mr = 114 g molГўВ€В’1).
mark_scheme:
Alternative 1
C2H5OH (l) + 3O2 (g) ГўВ†В’ 2CO2 (g) + 3H2O (l) / 1 mol ethanol produces 2 mol CO2
OR
C8H18 (l) + 12.5O2 (g) ГўВ†В’ 8CO2 (g) + 9H2O (l) / 1 mol octane produces 8 mol CO2Г‚В  [ГўВњВ”]


For 1 g of fuel:
Г‚В«Г‚В  1g 46 g mo l ГўВ€В’ 1 ГѓВ— 2 mol CO2 (g) =Г‚В» 0.04 Г‚В«mol CO2 (g)Г‚В» from ethanolГ‚В  [ГўВњВ”]

Г‚В«Г‚В  1g 114 g mo l ГўВ€В’ 1 ГѓВ— 8 mol CO2 (g) =Г‚В» 0.07 Г‚В«mol CO2 (g)Г‚В» from octaneГ‚В  [ГўВњВ”]

Г‚В 

Alternative 2
ratio of C in ethanol:octane is 2:8, so ratio in carbon dioxide produced per mole will be 1:4Г‚В  [ГўВњВ”]

ratio amount of fuel in 1 g =Г‚В  1 46 : 1 114 = 2.5:1Г‚В  [ГўВњВ”]

4 > 2.5 so octane produces more carbon dioxide
OR
ratio of amount of carbon dioxideГ‚В = 2.5:4 = 1:1.61 so octane produces more Г‚В«for combustion of same massГ‚В» [ГўВњВ”]

text_body:
This question is about biofuel.

Evaluate the use of biodiesel in place of diesel from crude oil.
mark_scheme:
Strength
Any one of:
less flammable Г‚В«than dieselГ‚В»Г‚В  [ГўВњВ”]

recycles carbon Г‚В«lower carbon footprintГ‚В»
OR
lower greenhouse gas emissionsГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

easily biodegradable Г‚В«in case of spillГ‚В»Г‚В [ГўВњВ”]

renewable
OR
does not deplete fossil fuel reservesГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

economic security/availability in countries without crude oilГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

Limitation
Any one of:
more difficult to ignite inside the engine Г‚В«than dieselГ‚В»Г‚В [ГўВњВ”]

more viscous Г‚В«than dieselГ‚В»Г‚В [ГўВњВ”]

lower energy content/specific energy/energy densityГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

uses food sources
OR
uses land that could be used for foodГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

Г‚В«production isГ‚В» more expensiveГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

less suitable in low temperaturesГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

increased NOx emissions for biodieselГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

greenhouse gases still producedГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

Г‚В 

Note:Г‚В Accept ГўВЂВњГ‚В«close toГ‚В» carbon neutralГўВЂВќ, ГўВЂВњproduce less greenhouse gases/CO2ГўВЂВќ.

Accept ГўВЂВњengines have to be modified if biodiesel usedГўВЂВќ as limitation.

Do not award marks for strength and limitation that are the same topic/concept.

text_body:
The regular rise and fall of sea levels, known as tides, can be used to generate energy.

State one advantage, other than limiting greenhouse gas emissions, and one disadvantage of tidal power.

Г‚В 

Advantage:

Disadvantage:
mark_scheme:
Advantage
Any one of:
renewable [ГўВњВ”]
predictable supply [ГўВњВ”]
tidal barrage may prevent floodingГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
effective at low speedsГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
long life-spanГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
low cost to runГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]


Disadvantage
Any one of:
cost of construction [ГўВњВ”]
changes/unknown effects on marine lifeГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
changes circulation of tides in the areaГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
power output is variableГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
limited locations where feasibleГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
equipment maintenance can be challengingГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
difficult to store energyГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

Г‚В 

Note:Г‚В Do not accept vague generalizations.

Do not accept economic issues for both advantage and disadvantage.

Do not accept sustainable.

Accept ГўВЂВњenergyГўВЂВќ or ГўВЂВњelectricityГўВЂВќ for ГўВЂВњpowerГўВЂВќ.

text_body: Outline the advantages and disadvantages of using biodiesel instead of gasoline as fuel for a car. Exclude any discussion of cost.
mark_scheme:
Advantages: [2 max]

renewable ГўВњВ”

uses up waste Г‚В«such as used cooking oilГ‚В» ГўВњВ”

lower carbon footprint/carbon neutral ГўВњВ”

higher flashpoint ГўВњВ”

produces less SOx/SO2
OR
less polluting emissions ГўВњВ”

has lubricating properties
OR
preserves/increases lifespan of engine ГўВњВ”

increases the life of the catalytic converter ГўВњВ”

eliminates dependence on foreign suppliers ГўВњВ”

does not require pipelines/infrastructure Г‚В«to produceГ‚В» ГўВњВ”

relatively less destruction of habitat compared to obtaining petrochemicals ГўВњВ”

Г‚В 

Accept ГўВЂВњhigher energy densityГўВЂВќ OR ГўВЂВњbiodegradableГўВЂВќ for advantage.


Disadvantages: [2 max]

needs conversion/transesterification ГўВњВ”

takes time to produce/grow plants ГўВњВ”

takes up land
OR
deforestation ГўВњВ”

fertilizers/pesticides/phosphates/nitrates Г‚В«used in production of cropsГ‚В» have negative environmental effects ГўВњВ”

biodiversity affected
OR
loss of habitats Г‚В«due to energy crop plantationsГ‚В» ГўВњВ”

cannot be used at low temperatures ГўВњВ”

variable quality Г‚В«in productionГ‚В» ГўВњВ”

high viscosity/can clog/damage engines ГўВњВ”


Accept ГўВЂВњlower specific energyГўВЂВќ as disadvantage.

Do not accept ГўВЂВњlower octane numberГўВЂВќ as disadvantageГўВЂВќ.

heterogeneous catalyst is in different phase than reactants AND homogeneous catalyst in same phase Г‚В  [ГўВњВ”]

homogeneous catalysts are Г‚В«generallyГ‚В» more selectiveГ‚В  Г‚В Г‚В  [ГўВњВ”]

text_body:
Determine the initial rate of reaction of limestone with nitric acid from the graph.

Show your working on the graph and include the units of the initial rate.
mark_scheme:
tangent drawn at time zero ГўВњВ”
g dayГўВ€В’1 ГўВњВ”
0.16 ГўВњВ”

Г‚В 

NOTE:Г‚В Accept other reasonable units for initial rate eg, mol dmГўВ€В’3 sГўВ€В’1, mol dmГўВ€В’3 minГўВ€В’1, g sГўВ€В’1 OR g minГўВ€В’1.

M3 can only be awarded if the value corresponds to the correct unit given in M2.
Accept values for the initial rate for M3 in the range: 0.13 ГўВ€В’ 0.20 g dayГўВ€В’1 OR 1.5 ГѓВ— 10ГўВ€В’6 g sГўВ€В’1 ГўВ€В’ 2.3 ГѓВ— 10ГўВ€В’6 g sГўВ€В’1 OR 7.5 ГѓВ— 10ГўВ€В’8 ГўВ€В’ 1.2 ГѓВ— 10ГўВ€В’7 mol dmГўВ€В’3 sГўВ€В’1 OR 4.5 ГѓВ— 10ГўВ€В’6 ГўВ€В’ 6.9 ГѓВ— 10ГўВ€В’6 mol dmГўВ€В’3 minГўВ€В’1 OR 9.0 ГѓВ— 10ГўВ€В’5 ГўВ€В’ 1.4 ГѓВ— 10ГўВ€В’4 g minГўВ€В’1 OR a range based on any other reasonable unit for rate.

Ignore any negative rate value.
Award [2 max] for answers such as 0.12/0.11 g dayГўВ€В’1, incorrectly obtained by using the first two points on the graph (the average rate between t = 0 and 1 day).
Award [1 max] for correctly calculating any other average rate.

ALTERNATIVE 1

plunger may stick/friction Г‚В«so pressure is greater than atmospheric pressureГ‚В»
OR
syringe may be tilted Г‚В«upГ‚В» so plunger moves less Г‚В«with gravity acting on plungerГ‚В»
OR
CO2 dissolved in waterГ‚В  [ГўВњВ”]
calculated rate lower [ГўВњВ”]

ALTERNATIVE 2
syringe may be tilted Г‚В«downГ‚В» so plunger moves more Г‚В«with gravity acting on plungerГ‚В»
OR
syringe is held in hand so gets warmer and gas expandsГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]
calculated rate higherГ‚В [ГўВњВ”]

Sodium thiosulfate reacts with hydrochloric acid as shown:

Na2S2O3ГўВЂВ‰(aq) + 2HClГўВЂВ‰(aq) ГўВ†В’ SГўВЂВ‰(s) + SO2ГўВЂВ‰(aq) + 2NaClГўВЂВ‰(aq) + H2OГўВЂВ‰(l)

The precipitate of sulfur makes the mixture cloudy, so a mark underneath the reactionГ‚В mixture becomes invisible with time.

Suggest two variables, other than concentration, that should be controlled whenГ‚В comparing relative rates at different temperatures.

depth/volume Г‚В«of solutionГ‚В» ГўВњВ”

colour/darkness/thickness/size/background of mark ГўВњВ”

intensity of lighting in the labГ‚В ГўВњВ”

Г‚В 

Accept same size flask.

Accept position of observation/person observing.

Accept same equipment/apparatus.

Do not accept catalyst/particle size/pressure/time.
